one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG768-recommended digital guided self-help for anorexia nervosa — a conditional recommendation with strict clinical pathway controls and safety requirements.
why_it_matters_locally: Eating disorder services face significant capacity pressures with long waiting times. Overcoming Anorexia Online offers a structured digital CBT-based self-help programme for appropriate patients within carefully defined clinical boundaries.
target_problem_statement: Long eating disorder service waiting times, limited access to evidence-based treatments for anorexia, and pressure on specialist eating disorder services.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Recommended during evidence generation for specific presentations

evidence_summary: NICE HTG768 recommendation during evidence generation. CBT-based guided self-help with clinical evidence base. Strict pathway controls and clinical inclusion/exclusion criteria are essential — NICE guidance specifies conditions of use.
evidence_strength_rationale: Evidence rationale not yet aligned to Servita scoring document.
live_sites: Limited NHS eating disorder services — pilot deployments

named_sites.name: Limited NHS eating disorder services — pilot deployments (sites not listed in catalogue)

patients_covered_note: Very limited NHS deployment at present. Evidence generation in early stages.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with Overcoming.
cyber_notes: Confirm data hosting, DPA terms and security certifications with Overcoming.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Overcoming.
target_patients: Adults with anorexia nervosa meeting NICE HTG768 eligibility criteria. Clinical assessment required before referral.
exclusions: NICE guidance specifies strict inclusion/exclusion criteria. Not appropriate for patients with severe anorexia, high medical risk, or those requiring intensive specialist input. Clinical safety review essential before deployment.
onboarding_detail: Clinician-led assessment and referral. Clinical safety review and pathway agreement required before deployment.
supplier_wrap: Overcoming provides the digital programme and reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Robust clinical pathway controls essential. Clinical safety review, local inclusion/exclusion criteria and escalation protocols must be established before deployment. Not suitable for unstructured self-referral.
training_note: Clinical training on inclusion/exclusion criteria and safety monitoring essential.
monitoring_model: Guided self-help with active clinical oversight. Not continuously monitored but requires regular clinical review.
monitoring_note: Guided self-help — not continuously monitored. Active clinical oversight of patient progress required within service pathway.
escalation_note: Clear escalation to specialist eating disorder services essential. Patients must have access to clinical support.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Robust escalation pathways to eating disorder services are mandatory before deployment.
implementation_prerequisites: Escalation pathway to specialist eating disorder services agreed
indicative_price_text: Pricing is typically structured as part of a broader SilverCloud eating disorders programme bundle. Indicative cost per patient is in the range of £80–£150. Bundle pricing with Overcoming Bulimia Online is available and generally more cost-effective. Commissioners with existing SilverCloud licences should check whether this programme can be added to their current agreement.
service_wrap_description: Digital programme content and platform access are included. Specialist clinical oversight must be provided locally — this is a mandatory requirement, not an optional enhancement. SilverCloud provides practitioner training and technical support but does not supply the specialist clinical workforce.
service_wrap_note: Clinical pathway wraparound is commissioner/provider responsibility.
contract_note: Contact Overcoming / SilverCloud for NHS procurement options.
procurement_notes: Available via the SilverCloud / Amwell platform. Strongly recommended to procure as part of the SilverCloud eating disorders programme suite alongside Overcoming Bulimia Online. Check existing local SilverCloud arrangements before separate procurement. Clinical governance requirements for this programme are more stringent than for other SilverCloud offerings due to the patient population. Evidence generation agreement with NICE may apply for listed technologies.
expected_benefit_note: Primary benefit is improved access to structured psychological support for patients who are waiting for, or stepping down from, specialist eating disorders treatment. May improve treatment readiness and engagement with specialist care. Direct cost savings should not be the primary commissioning rationale given clinical complexity and mandatory clinical oversight.
tariff_considerations: Eating disorders treatment is typically block-contracted. This programme is unlikely to generate direct tariff savings but may improve efficiency within existing specialist service budgets by supporting patients on waiting lists and facilitating earlier step-down from intensive treatment.
provider_income_note: This programme supplements rather than replaces specialist eating disorders provision. No direct provider income impact expected. May enable providers to manage waiting lists more effectively within existing resources.
roi_note: ROI modelling should be approached with caution. The primary value is clinical (earlier access to structured support, improved treatment readiness) rather than financial. Any cost benefit is likely to be indirect and long-term. Commissioners should not commission this programme primarily on a cost savings basis.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Specialist eating disorder clinical lead, defined inclusion/exclusion criteria, active safety monitoring protocol, minimum 30 patient pilot cohort.
source_summary: NICE HTG768, NICE NG69, Overcoming website.
confidence_note: Verify current assurance and pricing with Overcoming before procurement. Clinical safety review is mandatory before deployment.
clinical_safety_alert: This product has strict clinical inclusion/exclusion criteria and must only be deployed within a carefully designed pathway. Do not deploy without clinical safety review and specialist eating disorder service involvement.
